_rationale: tree is NOT registered in either Go or C++ MCP server (verified via tools/list iter-7). Both binaries return identical JSON-RPC error envelope: {error: {code: -32602, message: "unknown tool \"tree\""}}. Same 'tool missing on both' invariant as search_definitions/basic: stable tier paths absent on both → match; descriptor locks the symmetric unimplemented state. tree functionality is approximately covered by inspect_symbol with include=children, which exists on both sides — if a dedicated `tree` tool ever lands, this descriptor must flip to a real request shape and a richer tier list.

tiers._rationale_stable: Locks symmetric absence. If one side adds tree, result.content[] materialises and diff surfaces it.
